frameworks.name: dynamic belief updating
frameworks.one_line: evidence accumulation modulated to track hidden state changes

frameworks.name: pupil-linked arousal
frameworks.one_line: phasic pupil responses index dynamic gain on evidence samples

frameworks.name: psychosis proneness (continuum)
frameworks.one_line: subclinical psychotic experiences in community sample

core_question: Do individual differences in dynamic belief updating and phasic arousal relate to psychosis proneness?
key_assumptions: optimal updating in changing environments requires dynamic modulation of accumulation
phasic pupil responses index arousal-driven gain on evidence
psychosis lies on a continuum into the general population
manipulations: evidence samples within trials of a changing-environment task
individual differences in subclinical psychotic experiences
key_findings: psychosis-prone individuals show less flexible belief updating
diminished behavioral impact of late evidence samples in this group
smaller phasic pupil responses in psychosis-prone individuals
less reliable pupil encoding of computational updating variables
interpretations: arousal-linked gain modulation of evidence accumulation is altered along the psychosis continuum
